exoriaBeginnerGuide.title: Exoria Beginner Guide
exoriaBeginnerGuide.intro: Exoria is an open-world anime RPG where you grow by playing. Focus on codes first, then follow the core loop of fighting, exploring, questing, and boss hunting to level up fast.

steps.title: Claim your codes before entering the world
steps.description: Use the loading-screen Codes button first. Codes are the quickest source of free rolls, rerolls, and starter rewards in Exoria.

steps.title: Follow the core loop instead of roaming aimlessly
steps.description: Level by simply playing: fight enemies, explore cursed zones, do quests, or hunt bosses. That is the cleanest first-session priority order.

steps.title: Treat Purity as an early build choice
steps.description: Exoria's progression is tied to Purity. Your alignment changes the pacing of your build and the order of skill access from the very start.

steps.title: Use rolls and rerolls with a purpose
steps.description: Exoria codes help refine your character's race and abilities through rerolls. Use early free code rewards immediately rather than saving them for later.

steps.title: Open Trello when you need system detail
steps.description: For classes, weapons, races, accessories, dungeons, events, or bosses, the official Trello board is the fastest and most complete reference.

quickTips: Always claim codes first — they give free rolls before you even enter the world
Purity alignment affects skill unlock order and quest availability from the start
Race rerolling is most valuable in the early game before committing to a build
Boss drops and rare cards are covered in detail on the official Trello board
Discord is the fastest channel for new code drops and patch announcements

exoriaPurityPathGuide.title: Exoria Purity Path Guide
exoriaPurityPathGuide.intro: Purity Path is one of Exoria's defining progression systems. Your moral alignment shapes your skills, quest access, and how parts of the world react to your character.

faqs.question: What is the Exoria Purity Path?
faqs.answer: Purity Path is Exoria's moral alignment system. It is a core progression mechanic that shapes your skill unlock order, quest access, and how the game world responds to your character.

faqs.question: How do you change your Purity in Exoria?
faqs.answer: You shape your alignment by taking Purity Quests, which can be positive or negative. Your choices gradually move your character toward a cleaner or darker path over time.

faqs.question: What does Purity affect in Exoria?
faqs.answer: Purity affects how quickly skills unlock in the skill tree. Both skills and quests are gated behind your alignment progression, making it one of the most impactful early decisions.

faqs.question: Does the Exoria world react to your alignment?
faqs.answer: Yes. High purity may attract certain enemies, and some areas respond differently based on your alignment. The system frames your choice as cleansing the world versus embracing darker power.

exoriaClassesGuide.title: Exoria Classes Guide
exoriaClassesGuide.intro: Class choice in Exoria shapes how you fight, which skills you rush first, and how naturally your build connects into Purity Path progression, boss farming, and later-form upgrades.

cards.title: Sword Class
cards.description: Sword is the cleanest entry point for players who want direct close-range combat. Dedicated showcase coverage and a sword-skills upgrade make it the strongest class to feature near the top.
highlights: Dedicated Sword Class showcase on the official channel
Follow-up sword upgrade video confirms active iteration
Natural landing page for combos, upgrades, and boss-ready builds

cards.title: Aria Class
cards.description: Aria is the other major class path visible in official media. Multiple named ability clips are attached to it, giving users a clear reason to explore deeper class coverage.
highlights: Official channel references Aria Class directly
Named Aria clips include LightShot and Smash
Strong hub for skill previews, unlock order, and form tie-ins

cards.title: Class-to-Form Progression
cards.description: Class discussion in Exoria naturally extends into form progression. Dark Aria Form and Legendary Demon Form make later-stage class identity a real search intent.
highlights: Dark Aria Form is already highlighted in official shorts
Legendary Demon Form is officially showcased
Bridge from class pages into late-game progression

highlights: Positive and negative Purity Quests both affect progression

Best used as a class-planning section inside every major build page

exoriaRacesGuide.title: Exoria Races Guide
exoriaRacesGuide.intro: Race choice in Exoria is part of your long-term build identity. Rerolls, passive fit, and later-form progression all matter more when chosen early rather than corrected later.

cards.title: Race Fit Comes First
cards.description: Exoria ties race planning directly into long-term character growth. The strongest angle is helping users decide whether their first roll supports the build they want to play.
highlights: Race choice is part of build optimization from the start
Rerolls are valuable because race fit affects progression feel
Best structure: keep or reroll, class pairing, passive fit

cards.title: Rerolls and Race Correction
cards.description: Rerolls are one of the fastest ways players correct a bad opening setup, making race pages naturally conversion-heavy when paired with code and beginner advice.
highlights: Roll-focused rewards are central to Exoria code searches
Race pages work best when they link directly to reroll resources
Featured callout beside beginner and class modules

cards.title: Demon Transformation

highlights: Official Demon Transformation short is live on the channel
Fits darker-path users who want a strong identity page
Works well as a branch page under races and forms

cards.title: Legendary Demon Form

highlights: Officially showcased on the Exoria channel

cards.title: Dark Aria Form

Best linked from both race pages and Aria build pages

exoriaSkillTreeGuide.title: Exoria Skill Tree Guide
exoriaSkillTreeGuide.intro: Exoria's skill tree is built around moral alignment. Understanding Purity Path early unlocks better routes, wastes fewer rerolls, and lets you build with more intention from the start.

faqs.question: What actually changes your Exoria skill-tree pace?
faqs.answer: Your Purity Path changes how fast parts of the tree open up. Exoria uses positive and negative Purity Quests, and your alignment direction determines which skills and quest lines unlock earlier or later.

faqs.question: Why does the Exoria Purity Path matter more than a normal quest chain?
faqs.answer: Purity Path is not just flavor text. It directly affects progression by controlling access timing for skills and quests, so two players with the same playtime can end up on very different build timelines.

faqs.question: How should a new Exoria player plan the first stretch of the skill tree?
faqs.answer: Pick a class direction first, then commit your early questing around the alignment you want to push. This keeps your class route, race plan, and skill unlock order moving in the same direction.

faqs.question: How does alignment change the Exoria world itself?
faqs.answer: Exoria's world reacts to purity. Some areas respond to your alignment, and high purity can attract enemies, which turns the skill-tree system into a world-state system as well.

faqs.question: What are the best Exoria skill tree entry points for new players?
faqs.answer: Start with Purity Path basics, then choose between positive and negative quest routes. Prioritize early unlock nodes and use class, race, and form pages to guide your next step without guessing.

exoriaBossesGuide.title: Exoria Bosses Guide
exoriaBossesGuide.subtitle: Bosses are a core progression hook in Exoria — from named encounters like Malphas to rare drop farming and evolving abilities across boss dungeons.
exoriaBossesGuide.intro: Exoria frames boss hunting as part of the main leveling loop alongside quests and exploration. Boss encounters tie to rare drops and evolving abilities, making them more than one-time fights.

cards.title: Malphas
cards.description: The clearest named boss in current Exoria content, Malphas anchors the early boss experience and serves as the prime target for rare drop farming runs.

cards.title: Boss Dungeons
cards.description: Each major boss in Exoria is tied to a dungeon with unique mechanics, so understanding a boss means understanding the dungeon context around it.

cards.title: Rare Drop Farming
cards.description: Boss runs in Exoria are part of the reward loop — repeated clears connect to rare drops, secrets, and evolving abilities beyond a single progression checkpoint.

cards.title: Progression Role
cards.description: Bosses sit alongside quests, builds, and exploration as one of Exoria's core advancement paths, letting players choose how they level and grow.

exoriaDungeonsGuide.title: Exoria Dungeons Guide
exoriaDungeonsGuide.subtitle: Dungeons in Exoria are boss-focused spaces with unique mechanics — the main bridge between combat mastery, loot farming, and purity-based progression.
exoriaDungeonsGuide.intro: Exoria dungeons are not generic rooms to clear. Each connects directly to boss encounters, rare drops, and the purity-based progression system that shapes your skill growth.

cards.title: Unique Dungeon Mechanics
cards.description: Each Exoria dungeon is designed around specific boss encounters with distinctive mechanics, making them distinct from generic combat content.

cards.title: Part of the Leveling Loop
cards.description: Dungeon runs sit inside the normal growth loop alongside fighting, exploring, and quests, offering a structured path for dedicated Exoria progression.

cards.title: Reward Structure
cards.description: Exoria dungeons tie combat to rare drops, secrets, and evolving abilities — making completion value about more than just clearing content once.

cards.title: Purity-Linked Routing
cards.description: Your purity level affects dungeon routing and area reactions in Exoria, connecting dungeon runs directly to your alignment choices and build planning.

exoriaQuestsGuide.title: Exoria Quests Guide
exoriaQuestsGuide.subtitle: Quests in Exoria are tied to the Purity Path, skill unlock timing, and how the world reacts to your alignment choices — making every quest decision count.
exoriaQuestsGuide.intro: Exoria's quest system goes beyond standard missions. Purity Quests can be positive or negative, and your decisions shape how your skill tree opens and how the world responds to you.

steps.title: Use Quests as a Main Progression Lane
steps.description: Quests belong at the center of early Exoria progression alongside fighting, exploring, and boss hunting — giving structured experience and rewards from the start.

steps.title: Choose Your Purity Quests Carefully
steps.description: Purity Quests in Exoria can be positive or negative, and each choice pushes your alignment in a direction that affects skills, world reactions, and future quest availability.

steps.title: Track How Alignment Affects Skills
steps.description: Your Purity Path changes how quickly certain skills unlock in the Exoria skill tree, making quest choices carry real mechanical timing consequences.

steps.title: Watch for World Reactions
steps.description: High purity may attract enemies and some areas in Exoria respond to your purity level — making quest choices part of ongoing world-state progression, not just character stats.

exoriaCardsGuide.title: Exoria Cards Guide
exoriaCardsGuide.subtitle: Card collecting is a core part of Exoria's identity — sitting alongside bosses, skills, and secrets as part of the main progression fantasy.
exoriaCardsGuide.intro: Cards in Exoria are more than collectibles. They connect to skill growth, rare-drop hunting, and the broader progression systems that shape your build and playstyle.

cards.title: Card Collecting Confirmed
cards.description: Card collecting is one of Exoria's defining systems, making cards a central part of what the game is built around — not a side activity.

cards.title: Cards and Skill Unlocks
cards.description: Exoria pairs collecting cards with unlocking skills and discovering secrets, placing cards firmly inside the active progression loop from early play.

cards.title: Rare Card Farming
cards.description: Rare card drop rates and boss-item connections make farming a key part of the Exoria card system, rewarding repeated runs with meaningful progression.

cards.title: Cards in Build Planning
cards.description: Card collecting in Exoria connects naturally to classes, races, and progression routes — making the card page a bridge between all major build systems.

exoriaAccessoriesGuide.title: Exoria Accessories and Build Extras
exoriaAccessoriesGuide.subtitle: Exoria's accessory layer is a mix of artifacts, forms, auras, and reroll-supported build tuning. This module helps players understand what to chase after they settle on a core class path.
exoriaAccessoriesGuide.intro: Treat accessories as the layer that sharpens identity after class, race, and basic combat choices are already in place.

items.whatToTrack: Artifact farming is visible in active Exoria video coverage, including dedicated artifact sessions and class-plus-artifact showcase content.
items.playerValue: Artifacts work well as a chase layer for players who already understand their base class and want stronger progression targets.
items.bestUseCase: Prioritize artifact routes after your main class and weapon preference are locked in.

items.whatToTrack: Official Hadez Studio shorts publicly showcase Dark Aria Form and Aura and Legendary Demon Form.
items.playerValue: These are the clearest examples of Exoria's build-flavor layer beyond normal leveling and questing.
items.bestUseCase: Best for players who want their end-build identity to feel visually and mechanically distinct.

items.whatToTrack: Current Exoria code coverage repeatedly frames rerolls as a way to refine your race direction.
items.playerValue: Accessory goals become more meaningful after you stop rerolling and commit to a race identity.
items.bestUseCase: Settle your race first, then target accessories or forms that reinforce that identity.

items.whatToTrack: Public code guides also tie rerolls to abilities, not just race choice.
items.playerValue: Accessories and forms fit into a larger optimization loop instead of sitting in isolation.
items.bestUseCase: Finalize your class path and ability direction before hard-farming accessory-style upgrades.

items.whereToCheck: Official YouTube launch videos and the live Roblox listing

exoriaUpdate2PatchNotes.title: Exoria Update 2: Public Patch Timeline
exoriaUpdate2PatchNotes.subtitle: This module covers the Update 2 details visible through the live Roblox title, official launch videos, and current update coverage.
exoriaUpdate2PatchNotes.intro: Exoria is publicly labeled as a Free-to-Play experience running on Update 2, making the best treatment a short readable timeline of what changed and what players immediately felt.

items.label: Early Access Opens
items.details: The official Exoria launch video announced Early Access for March 7, 2026 after eight months of development.

items.label: Free-to-Play Launch
items.details: A later official launch video announced the wider Free-to-Play release for April 5, 2026.

items.label: Update 2 Goes Live
items.details: The live Roblox experience title now reads Exoria [FREE TO PLAY + Update 2], making Update 2 part of the game's main public-facing identity.

items.label: Player-Facing Changes
items.details: Current update coverage calls out 2 new classes and more quests as the clearest immediately visible Update 2 changes for active players.

exoriaReleaseDate.title: Exoria Release Date
exoriaReleaseDate.subtitle: Exoria's release story is split into an Early Access opening and a wider Free-to-Play launch. This module gives users the exact dates they usually search for first.
exoriaReleaseDate.intro: For search intent, what matters is not just one date but the full launch sequence. Exoria opened through Early Access, then expanded to Free-to-Play, and now runs Update 2 under Hadez Studio.

items.details: Both official launch videos frame Exoria as the result of eight months of development before opening access.
This was the first public access window announced by the developers.
This marked the broader public opening beyond the earlier access phase.
The Roblox listing currently presents the game as Exoria [FREE TO PLAY + Update 2] by @HADEZ_STUDIO.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Exoria

questions.question: What is Exoria on Roblox?
questions.answer: Exoria is a dark anime RPG on Roblox inspired by Blue Exorcist. Developed by HADEZ STUDIO, it features class-based combat, race progression, purity alignment, boss fights, and dungeon exploration.

questions.question: How do I get free rewards in Exoria?
questions.answer: Use the Codes menu from the loading screen or main menu. Active codes currently include ASCENSION4K and LUCKOVERDRIVE for free rolls, plus several others from the official game page.

questions.question: Is Exoria free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Exoria is free to play on Roblox. Update 2 is live and the game continues to receive new content from HADEZ STUDIO.

questions.question: What is the Purity Path in Exoria?
questions.answer: Purity Path is Exoria's moral alignment system. Your choices between positive and negative Purity Quests affect skill unlock speed, quest availability, and how the world reacts to your character.

questions.question: Where can I find the Exoria Trello board?
questions.answer: The official Exoria Trello board covers classes, weapons, races, accessories, dungeons, events, and bosses. It is the most complete reference for progression systems.

questions.question: When was Exoria last updated?
questions.answer: Exoria received Update 2 in April 2026, adding new content and improvements. The game is actively developed by HADEZ STUDIO on Roblox.
